subject: Ecology
title: Soil pH and Plant Communities
description: A quick win: understand Soil pH and Plant Communities.

hook.text: A small shift in soil pH can determine which plants thrive and which vanish.

buildup.text: Acidic soils favor blueberries, azaleas, and conifers. Alkaline soils suit lavender and clover.

discovery.text: pH controls nutrient availability—iron and manganese lock up in alkaline soil, starving plants.

twist.text: Acid rain can shift soil pH over decades, slowly replacing forests with acid-tolerant scrub.

climax.text: Liming acidic soils can restore lost species, but the recovery takes years.

punchline.text: The pH decides the garden before the gardener.

quiz.question: How does pH affect plant nutrition?
options: It controls which nutrients are available to roots
It has no effect on plants
